title: echinops spinosus subsp. fontqueri
intro: Echinops spinosus subsp. fontqueri, also known as the Globe Thistle, is a subspecies of the Echinops spinosus plant, a member of the sunflower family, characterized by its spiny stems and globe-shaped purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Echinops spinosus subsp. fontqueri include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and even exhibit antiviral properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
